name: Linzer Cookies
recipeIngredient: 3 1/3 cups Very finely ground Hazelnuts or almonds
recipeInstructions:
From the White House pastry Chef - Albert Kumin:
Crisp nut cookies sandwiched together with a raspberry filling, which peeks through the cutout in the top cookie.
NOTE: When unsifted flour is called for, fluff up the flour in the sack or container with a fork before measuring. Preheat oven to 350°F.
1. Beat butter and sugar in a large bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in egg, egg yolk, lemon rind, and vanilla.
3. Stir in nuts, flour, baking powder, and cinnamon, blending well to make a stiff dough. Wrap in wax paper and chill several hours or overnight.
4. Cut dough in half (refrigerate the other half).
5. Roll out each half of dough between sheets of wax paper to slightly less than 1/4 inch thickness.
6. Remove top sheet of wax paper.
7. With a 2-inch round cookie cutter, cut out as many circles from the dough as possible. Carefully remove circles from the bottom sheet of wax paper and place cookies on ungreased cookie sheets.
8. Refrigerate scraps of dough for second rolling.
9. Repeat with the other half of the dough, cutting out an equal number of 2-inch circles as from the first batch.
10. Place these on ungreased cookie sheets; cut out centers of each cookie in the second batch.
11. Use any remaining scraps of dough for a second rolling.
12. Roll scraps of dough out for a second rolling and cut out an equal number of solid circles and circles with open centers.
13. Bake in a 350°F oven for 12 minutes, or until edges of cookies are golden. Remove cookie sheets from the oven and let stand 1 minute.
14. Using a wide spatula, remove cookies to wire racks and cool.
15. Heat raspberry jam in a small saucepan.
16. Spread each solid cookie completely with a thick layer of hot preserves.
17. Top each with a cut-out cookie and gently press together to make a "sandwich".
18. Place on a wire rack.
19. Sprinkle tops of cookies with confectioners' sugar.
20. Spoon a dab of preserves in the opening of each cookie; let preserves set slightly.
Makes 4 dozen cookies. Family Circle, 12/79
